translations.source: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO); Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF); Unitaid; United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women);
translations.reference: I-SDG3 GAPichaza ukuthi ukusayina kwama ejensi angu-13 kuzithatha kanjani izindlela ezintsha zokusebenza, ukwakha phezu kokubambisana okukhona okuphumelele, ukubambisana okuhambisana nokweseka kwabo ezinhlelweni zezwe namasu okungawezwe futhi aholwa yilo. Ukusayidwa kuSDG3 GAP kuyiGavi ubudlelwane bomgomo; Indawo yezezimali zomhlaba yabesifazane izingane nenstha (GFF); uxhasomali lomhlaba lokulwa kengculaza,isifo sofuba nomalaleveva(Global Fund); I-International Labour Organisation (ILO); Uhlelo lokuhlanganyela lweUnited Nations lwe HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); I-United Nation Development Fund (UNDP); I-United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); I-UnitedNations Children Fund(UNICEF); Unitaid; I-United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women);

translations.source: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023. Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning.
translations.reference: Imiphumela yocwaningo olusha ikhombisa izinhlelo zempilo ziqala ukuba ngcono esikhathini sobhubhane Ngemva kweminyaka emithathu yobhubhane lweCOVID-19 izinhlelo zempilo emazweni seziqale ukukhombisa izinkomba ezinkulu zokuvuseleleka kwezinhlelo zempilo, ngokweWHO umbiko wesikhashana emzuliswaneni wesine wocwaningo lephalisi yomhlaba ekuqhubekeni kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile ngesikhathi sobhubhane lweCOVID-19: Lwezi 2022-Masingana 2023. Ngokuqala kuka-2023 amazwe abike ukwenzeka kokwehla kokuphazamiseka ekulethweni kwendlela yemisebenzi yezempilo,kodwa kwavezwa isidingo sokutshala kokungalungiswa nokubekezela kakhulu kwikusasa. Kulamazwe angu-139 aphendule kumzuliswane wesine wokuzwakala kocwaningo lweWHO, amazwe abike ukuqhubeka kokuphazamiseka emisebenzini yekota esilinganisweni. Emazweni angu-84 lapho ukuhlaziya okudumile kungenzeka,iphesenti lemisebenzi ephazamisekile yehlile ngesilinganiso esisuka ku-56% ngoNtulikazi kuya kuMandulo 2020 ngo-23 % ngoLwezi 2022-Masingana 2023. Ukuqhubeka kokuphazamiseka kungenxa yakho kokubili ukufuneka nokusaplaya izinkinga eziseceleni, okufaka amazinga aphansi okunakekelwa kwezempilo emphakathini okudingayo kanye nomkhawulo wokubakhona kwabasebenzi bezempilo nezinsizakusebenza ezinye zokunakekelwa kwezempilo njengokuvulwa kwemitholampilo noma ukubakhona kwesitoko semithi nemikhiqizo. "Izindaba ezamukelwayo ukuthi izinhlelozempilo emzaweni amaningi ziqala ukuvuselela imisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile ezigidini zabantu abawugejile ngesikhathi sobhubhane," kusho uDkt Rudi Eggers, Umqondisi weWHO weMisebenzi yeZempilo yoKuhlanganisiwe. "Kodwa sidinga ukuqinisekisa ukuthi wonke amazwe ayaqhubeka nokuvala izikhala ukuvuselela imisebenzi yezempilo,nokusebenzisa izifundo abazifundile ukwakha izinhlelo zempilo ezizilungiselele nokubekezela kwekusasa". Izimpawu zokuqala eziqaphelekayo ekuvuseleleni Kulolu cwaningo olusha,amazwe ambalwa abike ngokungenhloso isikalo sasemuva sokuthola kuzinkundla zonke umsebenzi wokuletha nokusebenza kwezempilo yomphakathi okubalulekile kusukela 2020-2021 ukubikwa kukhombisa ukubaluleka kwegxathu kubuyelwe emazingeni angaphambi kobhubhane ukulethwa kokusebenza nokusebenza okukhulu kwezinhlelo.
translations.hypothesis: नयाँ अनुसन्धानले देखाउँछ कि प्रणालीले महामारीबाट निको हुन थालेको।यस अनुसार, विश्व स्वास्थ्य सङ्ग (WHO) ले "चौथो विश्वव्यापी सर्वेक्षणको अनु отчет", जुन "कोविड-19 महामारीको समयमा आवश्यक स्वास्थ्य सेवाहरूको निरन्तरता", नोभेम्बर 2022 - जनवरी 2023, ले जनाएको छ, स्वास्थ्य प्रणालीहरूले महामारीको तीन वर्षयता, पहिलो मुख्य संकेतहरूले निको हुनु थाल्यो।यस अनुसार, 2023 सुरुमा, देशहरूले नियमित स्वास्थ्य सेवाहरूको वितरणमा कमी अनुभव गरेको रिपोर्ट, तर भविष्यका लागि निको हुनु र मजबुत प्रतिरोधमा लगानी आवश्यक रहेको उल्लेख गरे।मध्ये 139 देशहरूले WHO ले गरेको चौथो सर्वेक्षणमा प्रतिक्रिया दिने, देशहरूले लगभग एक चौथाई सेवाहरूमा लगातार समस्या अनुभव गरेको रिपोर्ट।84 देशहरूमा, जहाँ प्रवृत्ति विश्लेषण सम्भव, औसतमा सेवाहरूको प्रतिशत घट्यो, 2020-2021 मा जुलाई-सितम्बरमा 56% देखि 2022 नोभेम्बर - 2023 जनवरी मा 23%. लगातार समस्याहरूले दुवै माग र आपूर्ति पक्षका कारकहरू समावेश छन्, जसमा समुदायहरूमा स्वास्थ्य सेवाको कम स्तर, साथै स्वास्थ्य работकहरू र औषधिको र अन्य स्वास्थ्य सेवा स्रोतहरूको सीमित उपलब्धता समावेश छ। "हामीले स्वागत योग्य खबर हो कि अधिकांश देशहरूले महामारीको समयमा सेवाहरूबाट वञ्चित भएका, लाखौं मानिसका लागि आवश्यक स्वास्थ्य सेवाहरू फर्काउन थालेका छन्," भन्यो WHO ले एकीकृत स्वास्थ्य सेवाहरूको निर्देशक, डा. रुडी एगर। "तर, हामीले यो अन्तरलाई पूर्ण रूपमा भरपर्छ, र भविष्यका लागि, थप तयारी र मजबुत स्वास्थ्य प्रणालीहरू निर्माण गर्न, सिकेका पाठहरू प्रयोग गर्न आवश्यक छ". पहिलो उल्लेखनीय निको हुनुका संकेत यस नयाँ सर्वेक्षणमा, कम देशहरूले 2020-2021 मा रिपोर्ट गरिएको, सेवा वितरणका सबै प्लेटफर्महरू र आवश्यक सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य कार्यहरूमा पहुँचलाई जान्दो रूपमा घटाई, सेवा वितरण र व्यापक प्रणालीको कार्यमा फर्किनको लागि एक महत्त्वपूर्ण कदम देखाएको छ।

translations.source: By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations. Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery.
translations.reference: Ekupheleni kuka-2022, amazwe amaningi abike izimpawu ezingacacile zokuvuselelwa kokusebenza okufaka ukusebenza kwezocansi, ukuzala, abakhullwe, abazelwe, izingane nezempilo yentsha, ukudla okunempilo ukugomma,izifo ezithelelanayo (okufaka umalaleveva, iHIV, iTB nezinye izifo ezithelelana ngokocansi) izifo ezidumile ezinganakiwe,izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo,ukuphathwa kwengqondo, okwengqondo nesifo sokusebenzisa izidakamiza, ukunakekelwa kwabantu abadala nokunakekelwa okulekelelayo nokwesiko. Inombolo yamazwe abika ukuphazamiseka ohlelweni lwawo lesaplayi sheni ezweni okungafika kuhhafu (29 ka-59 wamazwe aphendulayo) okunganekota (18 wa-66 wamazwe) aphedulayo kulonyaka odlule. Ngaphandle kwezinkomba zokuvuselela,ukuphazamiseka komsebenzi kuyaqhubeka emazweni onke namazinga emali engenayo kuzona zonke izinhlelo soomsebenzi wokulatha nokulandelela umsebenzi ezindaweni. Amazwe aphinde abhekane nokunyuka komsebenzi okumele wenziwe - ikakhulukazi emisebenzini yokubheka, ukuxilonga nokwelapha izifo okungaxhunyanwa ngazo - okungaholela kumiphumela engemihle uma abantu bebambezeleka ukuthola ukunakekelwa ngesikhathi. Ukuvuselelwa kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile kubucayi ngenxa yokuphazamiseka - okufaka imisebenzi yokugqugquzela ezempilo, ukuvinjwa kwezifo, ukwelapha, ukuvuselelwa kwesimilo nephaliyeshini - kungaba nokukhulu umthelela ohlukene wezempil nezinga lomuntu ukunobhubhane qobo ikakhulukazi kubantu babuthaka. Ukuhlanganisa imisebenzi yeCOVID-19 emisebenzini yezempilo ebalulekile elinye igxathu elibalulekile eliya ekuvuselelweni kohlelo noguquko,amazwe amaningi asebe nenqubekela phambili ekuhlanganiseni imisebenzi yeCOVID-19 endleleni yokuletha imisebenzi yezempilo. Cishe u-80%-90% wamazwe ahlanganyele ngokuphelele ekugonyweni kweCOVID-19 ukuxilonga nemisebenzi yokuphathwa kwecala kanye nemisebenzi yesimo sangemuva kweCOVID-19 endleleni yomsebenzi wokuletha. Kuyafana amazwe amaningi (u-80% wamazwe awu-83 aphendulile) abike okungenani okuncane ekukhuphukeni kokuthola amathuluzi abalulekile eCovid-19 (isibonelo ukuxilongwa kweCOVID-19, ukwelaphangxube, imigomo namathuluzi okuzivikela siqu - PPE), ngezinkinga zokusebenza kwezempilo nokwentuleka koxhasomali okumele izivimbo ezijwayeleke kakhulu. Ukuqhubeka kokusekwa kuyadingeka ukuvuselela,ukubekezela nokuzilungiselela Amazwe amaningi aseqalile asebenzisa asebekufundile ngesikhathi sobhubhane lweCOVID-19 okufaka ngezikhungo zenamba yokusbenza kokusha ukuphazamisa amasu endleleni yokulethwa kwezempilo.
translations.hypothesis: यस 2022, अधिकांश देशले सेवापुनर्स्थापनाकाका संकेतहरू, सहित सेवाहरू जसमा समावेश छन्: यौन, प्रजनन, मातृ, नवजात, बालबालिको र किशोर स्वास्थ्य; पोषण; खोप; संक्रामक रोगहरू (जसमध्ये मलेरिया, एचआईभी, टीबी, र अन्य यौन मार्ने संक्रमण); उपेक्षित उष्णकटिबंधीय रोगहरू; गैर-संक्रामक रोगहरू; मानसिक, तंत्रिका र नशा उपयोग विकारोंको व्यवस्थापन; वृद्धहरूबु, र परम्परागत र/वा पूरक स्याहार। संख्याको देशहरूले उनीहरूको राष्ट्रिय आपूर्तिके प्रणालीमा समस्या रिपोर्ट गरेका, घट्यो देखि लगभग आधा (29 मध्ये 59 प्रतिक्रिया दिने देशहरू) मा लगभग एक चौथाई (18 मध्ये 66 प्रतिक्रिया दिने देशहरू) मा, मा गत वर्ष। जसुका संकेतहरू, सेवाहरूमा समस्या, कायम छ, सबै क्षेत्रहरू र आय स्तरहरू, र अधिकांश सेवा वितरण सेटिंग र ट्रैसेर सेवा क्षेत्रहरूमा। देशहरूले पनि सेवाहरूमा वृद्धि - विशेषतः गैर-संक्रामक रोगहरूको छानबीन, निदान र उपचार - को सामना, जेले नकारात्मक परिणामहरू निम्नास्न सक्छ, किनकि मानिसहरूले समयजनक स्याहार प्राप्त गर्न ढिला। आवश्यक स्वास्थ्य सेवा वितरणको पुन:स्थापना महत्त्वपूर्ण हो, किनभने - जसमा स्वास्थ्यको प्रोत्साहन, रोग रोकथाम, निदान, उपचार, पुनर्वसन र आराम - समावेश छन् - समस्याहरू, महामारी भन्दा धेरै हानिकारक प्रभावहरू, विशेष रूपमा कमजोर जनसंख्यामा, उत्पन्न हुन सक्छ। COVID-19 सेवाहरूलाई आवश्यक स्वास्थ्य सेवाहरूमा एकीकृत गनु। अर्को महत्त्वपूर्ण कदम प्रणालीको पुन:स्थापना र संक्रमण, अधिकांश देशहरूले COVID-19 सेवाहरूलाई नियमित स्वास्थ्य सेवा वितरणमा एकीकृत गर्न प्रगति गरेका छन्। लगभग 80-90% देशहरूले पूर्ण रूपमा COVID-19 खोप, निदान र केस व्यवस्थापन सेवाहरू, साथै COVID-19 पछिको अवस्था सेवाहरू, नियमित सेवा वितरणमा एकीकृत गरेका छन्। तथास, अधिकांश देशहरू (80% मध्ये 83 प्रतिक्रिया दिने देशहरू) कम से कम एक बाधा रिपोर्ट गरेका छन्, जसमा आवश्यक COVID-19 उपकरणहरू (जसमध्ये COVID-19 निदान, उपचार, खोप र व्यक्तिगत सुरक्षा उपकरण - PPE) पुग्न, स्वास्थ्य कर्मचारी समस्या र धन अभाव, सबैभन्दा सामान्य बाधा हुन्। पुन:स्थापना, लचीलापन र तयारीको लागि थप समर्थन आवश्यक छ। अधिकांश देशहरूले COVID-19 महामारीको क्रममा सीखीस, सहित, विभिन्न नवीन सेवा विघटन निवारण रणनीतिको संस्थागतकरण, नियमित स्वास्थ्य सेवा वितरणमा लागू गरेका छन्।

translations.source: These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action. While pulse surveys have limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and delivers information rapidly. The term “country” should be understood to include all countries, territories and areas. Trend analyses was completed based on responses from 84 countries, territories, or areas that responded to at least one survey section in all four pulse survey rounds.
translations.reference: Lokhu kufaka ukubekwa kwezindlela zokwelapha ngefoni,ukugqugquzela ukunakekelwa usekhaya nokungenelela kokuzinakekela, izindlela zokuqinisa ukubakhona kwabasebenzi bezempilo, amandla nezindlela zokweseka, ukwenza kabusha ekuthengeni nokuletha imithi namasaplayi ezinye izindlela zokuxhumana nomphakathi ukubambisana nabanikezeli bomkhakha ozimele nowomphakathi. Amakota amathathu ezwe abaike ukufakwa koxhasomali olwengeziwe oluya ekuvuselelweni kohlelo lwesikhathi eside, ukubekezela nokuzilungiselela. Amazwe agcizelele isidingo sokwesekwa kweWHO ekubhekaneni nezinkinga ezisele ngokwengqikithi yeCOVID-19 nangale,ikakhulukazi okujwayele ukuhlobana nokuqinisa ukusebenza kwezempilo, ukwakha amandla okubheka imisebenzi yezempilo, ukwakha ukunakekelwa kwezempilo kokuqala okuncike kuzindlela zokunakekela, ukubusa, inqubomgomo nokuhlela nokuhlela ezezimali noxhasomali. Inothi kubahleli: Emzuliswaneni wesine wocwaningo lwephasile yomhlaba weWHO, amazwe angu-222, izindawo bamenywa ukuba baphendule kucwaningo oluncike kuwebhu eqinisikesiwe phakathi kukaLwezi 2022 noMasingana 2023. Ucwaningo lwesine lulandele kucwaningo lwephasile edlule yango-2020 no2021 lweWHO: uMzuliswano 1 (Nhlaba-Mandulo 2020) uMzuliswano 2 (Masingana-Ndasa 2021) noMzuliswano 3 (Lwezi-Zibandlela 2021) okwakhombisa ukukhulu bokuthi ubhubhane lalukhahlameza ukuqhubeka kwemisebenzi yezempilo ebalulekile nokuthi amazwe enza kanjani. Ngesikhathi ucwaningo lwephasile lunemikhawulo njengokubika ukuchema nokumela,amandla alomzamo ukuthi mukhulu futhi uletha ulwazi ngokuphindekile. Itemu "izwe" kumele liqondwe ukuthi lifaka wonke amazwe, imingcele nezindawo. Abahlaziya okudumile kwaqedwa kuncike ezimpendulweni eziphuma emazweni angu-84, abahlukumezi noma izindawo ezaphendula okungenani kusahluko socwaningo olulodwa kuyona yonke imizuliswano emine yocwaningo.
translations.hypothesis: यी समावेश्‌ छन्: कार्यान्वयनको उपयोग, प्रोत्साहनको उपयोग, प्रावधान, तथा समर्थन, नवीन समाधानहरू, र साझेदारिता। तीन चौथाइको देशहरूले थप रूपान्तरण, लचीलापन, र तयारीको लागि स्रोत निर्देशन। देशहरूले WHO समर्थनको आवश्यकता व्यक्त गरे, ताकि समाधान गर्न सकू COVID-19 परिप्रेक्ष्य र पछि, मुख्य रूपमा स्वास्थ्यकर्मी, स्वास्थ्य सेवा निगरानी क्षमता, प्राथमिक स्वास्थ्य सेवा-आधारित सेवा, सुशासन, नीति र योजना, र वित्तीय योजना र स्रोत। नोट: लेखकों: पहिलो WHO विश्वव्यापी सर्वेक्षण, 222 देशहरू, क्षेत्रहरू, र क्षेत्रहरूलाई नोभेम्बर 2022 र जनवरी 2023 बीच मानकीकृत वेब-आधारित सर्वेक्षणमा प्रतिक्रिया दिनका लागि आमन्त्रण गरिएको थियो। चौथो सर्वेक्षण WHO मा 2020 र 2021 मा गरिएको सर्वेक्षणहरू पछ्यायो: पहिलो (मे-सितябрь 2020), दोस्रो (जनवरी-मार्च 2021), र तेस्रो (नोभेम्बर-डिसेम्बर 2021), जे कसले महामारीले आवश्यक स्वास्थ्य सेवाहरूको सुसंगततामा कति प्रभाव लमला, र देशहरू कति कार्य गरिरहेको छ। यद्यपि, सर्वेक्षणहरूले सीमितताहरू जस्तै, रिपोर्टिंग पूर्वाग्रह र प्रतिनिधित्व, यो प्रयासको शक्ति हो, यसले पूर्णता र जानकारीको छिटपुट वितरण प्रदान गर्दछ। शब्द "देश" समावेश सबै देशहरू, क्षेत्रहरू, र क्षेत्रहरूलाई बुझनु पर्दछ। प्रवृत्ति विश्लेषण पूर्ण भयो, आधारमा 84 देशहरू, क्षेत्रहरू, वा क्षेत्रहरूले चार सर्वेक्षण राउन्डहरूमा कम से कम एक सर्वेक्षण अनुभागमा प्रतिक्रिया दिइएको थियो।

translations.source: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights. We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health.
translations.reference: Isitatimende sokubungaza ukuphela kweminyaka engu-50 yeHRP: iSweden ne-United Kingdom of Great Britain neNorthen Ireland Ukubamba isimo seNqubomgomo neKomidi lokuXhumana (PCC) Amalungu abungaza ukuphela kuka-50 HRP nokuzuza okuningi, iSweden neUnited Kingdom of Great Britain neNorthern Ireland ukufunda ukuqapheleka okulandelayo kokwamukela nokuhalalisa emhlanganweni wesi-36 weHRP PCC. Isizukulwane sethu sesibone inqubekela phambili enkulu kusayensi nezobuchwepheshe ukuthi konke okuqhubekayo kwesizukulwane ndawonye, nokubona okwemfundo okumbalwa,ezobuchwepheshe, ezokwelapha noguquko lwenhlalakahle luqhutshwa ukuguquka kokuvikela kulandelwa ukwenzangcono kwezempilo yokuzala, ukwenza kabusha kwezempilo kanye namalungelo nokulingana ngokobulili. Kepha ukucabanga izingqinamba emkhakheni weSRHR uqhubeke futhi kuphikiswane,okunye ukuqhubeka kusembuzweni futhi namhlanje. Sincoma abasebenzi beHRP ababambisani nabeseki kwabakutholile okuningi kuze kube yinamhlanje nokubona izinto ezinkulu ezenzekile ngesikhathi esinguhhafu wekhulunyaka ekukhiqizeni ubufakazi obeseka ukungena okuphelele nokulinganayo kwezempilo namalungelo ezocansi nokuzala. Singathanda ukuveza imikhakha emikhulu emithathu yokuzuziwe Okokuqala ucwaningo olusezingeni eliphezulu lweHRP lube nomthelela obonakalayo kunqubomgomo yezempilo yomphakathi,amazinga, nokwenza emhlabeni wonke okubhekene nezidingo zokuguquka kusibalo sabantu esahlukene. Okwesibili HRP iqapheleke ngeHRP Alliance, isiqinise izikhungo zocwaningpo endaweni nasemhlabeni,kwavumela abacwaningi abaningi ukuthi bathole amakhono okucwabinga ukuqinisekisa ubunhalo obuphezulu bocwaningo lweSRHR ukuthi liqhubeke futhi lunyuke esikhathini esizayo. Okwesithathu,umhlaba uhlelwa yizinkinga, ukuxabana nokunyusa ukuqaba ekulinganeni kobulili, iHRP isize ukubamba isisekelo samalungelo abasefazane nezingane nabantu ababuthaka. I-HRP ihlala iyithuluzi elibalulekile kuhlelo lweUnited Nations yocwaningo lokhukhiqiza kubantu nokuqhubeka nokuba umholi ovelele kwezempilo yezocansi nokuzala emhlabeni.
translations.hypothesis: घोषणाकोత్సविङको लागि: HRP ले मनाएका 50 औं वर्ष: स्वीडन र ग्रेट ब्रिटेन र उत्तरी आयरल्याण्ड।यसले, स्वीडन र ग्रेट ब्रिटेन र उत्तरी आयरल्याण्ड, नीति र समन्वय समिति (PCC) सदस्यहरूले HRP ले मनाएका 50 औं वर्ष र धेरै उपलब्धहरूलाई प्रतिबिम्बित गर्न,ले 36 औं भेलाको HRP PCC मा, निम्न प्रशंसा र बधाई नोट पढे. हाम्रो पुनरेशनले विज्ञान र प्रौद्योगिकीमा धेरै प्रगति देखेको छ, जसमा सबै पूर्व पुनरेशनहरू मिलेर, साथै गर्भनिरोधक क्रान्तिले नेतृत्व गरेको, र प्रजनन स्वास्थ्य, स्वास्थ्य नवोन्मेषहरू, तथा अधिकार र लिंग समानताको विकास समावेश छन्. तथापि, SRHR क्षेत्रमा महत्वपूर्ण चुनौतीहरू अझै कायम, र, सम्भवतः, केही प्रगति आज पुनः प्रश्न गरिन्छ. हामी HRP कर्मचारी, साझेदारहरू, र समर्थकहरूलाई, आजसम्म हासिल गरिएका धेरै उपलब्धहरूमा, साथै पूर्ण र समान पहुँचको लागि यौन र प्रजनन स्वास्थ्य र अधिकारहरूलाई समर्थन गर्ने प्रमाणहरू उत्पन्न गरेको आधा शताब्दीमा हासिल गरिएका विशाल प्रगति, बधाई दिन्छौं. हामी तीन मुख्य प्रकारका उपलब्धहरूमा ध्यान केन्द्रित गर्न चाहन्छौं: पहिलो, HRP ले उच्च-गुणवत्तायुक्त अनुसन्धानले, विश्वव्यापी रूपमा, विभिन्न जनसंख्याको विकसित आवश्यकताहरूलाई हेर्दै, सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य नीति, मान र अभ्यासमा मूर्त प्रभाव लेशे. दोस्रो, HRP, विशेष रूपमा HRP गठजोړ, स्थानीय र विश्वव्यापी रूपमा अनुसन्धान संस्थालाई सुदृढीकरण गरेको छ, र, धेरै अनुसन्धानकर्ताहरूलाई, उच्च-गुणवत्ता SRHR अनुसन्धानले भविष्यमा पनि जारी रहन, वास्तवमा, वृद्धि हुन, सुनिश्चित गर्ने अनुसन्धान कौशल हासिल गर्न मद्दत गरेको छ. तेस्रो, एक जताततै संकट, संघर्ष, र लिंग समानताविरुद्ध बढिरहेको प्रतिरोधले, HRP ले महिलाहरू र कमजोर जनसंख्याका मूल अधिकारहरू कायम गर्न मद्दत गरेको छ. HRP, संयुक्त राष्ट्र प्रणाली अन्तर्गत, मानव प्रजननमा अनुसन्धानको मुख्य साधन रह्यो, र, विश्वव्यापी यौन र प्रजनन स्वास्थ्यमा उत्कृष्ट नेता रह्यो.

translations.source: HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.reference: HRP nababambisani bayo sebesisondeze kusithembiso sango-1994 KuNkomfa Yamazwe angaphandle Esibalweni Sabantu neNtuthuko, futhi isimemezelo sibeka amalungelo abantu ikakhulukazi abesifazane namantombazane phakathi nokugcineka kwentuthuko. Sifisa ukuzwakalisa ukubonga nokujabula kwethu kunobhala weHRP, ababambisani, kanye nabaxhasi kanye nohlelokuxhumana lomhlaba ezizindeni zokubambisana nongoti abasiza ukwenza isiqiniseko ukuthi imizamo inikela ekwenzeni umehluko kubantu besifazane, amadoda, izingane, intsha, nemiphakathini eyahlukene iHRP izimisele ngokusiza. Ngokubambela amalunga ePCC sivumeleni siqinisekise ukubambisana kwethu nani ukweseka okuzuziwe kweHRP ekuphumeleleni okuncike ku-ajenda 2030.
translations.hypothesis: HRP र यसका साझेदारहरूले, ले हामीलाई 1994 मा आयोजना गरिएको अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय सम्मेलनमा, र विकासमा, विशेषतः महिला र बालिका, अधिकारहरूलाई स्थायी विकासको केन्द्रबिन्दुमा राख्ने आह्वान, ले नजिक ल्याए। हामी चाहन्छौ व्यक्त गर्न, हाम्रो आभारी र धन्यवाद, कयेशेcretariat, यसका साझेदारहरू, सह-आयोजकहरू र, र विभिन्न केन्द्र र विशेषज्ञहरूको, जो HRP को प्रयासले, महिला, पुरुष, बच्चाहरू, किशोरहरू र विभिन्न समुदायहरूमा, सकारात्मक प्रभाव पार्न मद्दत,। नाममा PCC सदस्यहरू, कृपया, हामीले HRP को उपलब्धिलाई, समर्थन गर्न, हाम्रो साझेदारी,पुनः पुष्टि, जुन 2030 Agenda को सफलतामा निर्भर,

translations.source: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions. The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.reference: I-WHO yethula umzamo omusha wokwenza ngcono ukuzilungiselela kobhubhane Ukusiza amazwe ukuthi azilungisele okungcono kwekusasa lobhubhane,WHO yethule umzamo namahlanje ozonikeza imiyalelo yokuhlela ngokuhlanganisa ngokuphendula kunoma iyiphi iphathojini yokuphefumula njengomkhuhlane noma ikhorona. I-Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, noma iPRET, ihlanganisa amathuluzi akamuva nezindlela zokufunda okwabelwana ngazo kanye nesenzo esiyiqoqo esisungulwe phakathi nobhadane lweCOVID-19 nezinye izimo eziphuthumayo zakamuva zezempilo zomphakathi. Ngomzamo iWHO izosebenzisa indlela yokudlulisela ukuyala amazwe ekuhleleni ubhubhane,ngoba kukhona amandla amaningi namandla amaningi afanayo kumaqoqo ephathojini. I-PRET iphendula isimemezelo somuyalelo wezobuchwepheshe nokwesekwa kokugqugquzelwa nokuqiniswa kokuhlanganisa ukuzilungiselela nokuphendula,njengova kuveziwe kuzinqumo zeWorld Health Assembly. Ububhane lweCOVID-19 nezinye izimo eziphuthumayo zezempilo zikhombisile ukuthi amazwe adinga ukuthi alungele ukubhekana nezinsongo zezifo ezithathelwanayo, ngezinhlelo zokuzilungiselela ezilungiselelwe ngesandla kanye nokuxhumana okungcono nokusebenzisana neminye imikhakha efana nezolimo. "Ukuzilungiselela, ukuvikela nemisebenzi yokuphendula akumele kube yisifundazwe somkhakha wezempilo kuphela," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Njengeziphuthumayo zempilo zinomthelela kumikhakha eminingi, ngakho nokuzilungiselela kwethu nemizamo yokuphendula mayilingane nemikhakah, izinhlobo namagciwane. Kubucayi futhi ukuthi ukukhuluma komphakathi nokulingana kuphakathi kwemizamo yethu, ikakhulukazi kulabo bantu abagcindezelekile futhi abasengcupheni enkulu." I-PRET iwuguquko endleleni yeWHO ekuzilungiseleni kobhubhane ngokusebenzisa indlela yelensi yokudlulisela, ukunokugxila ezifweni ezithile.
translations.hypothesis: WHO सुरु नया पहलको लागि सुधार महामारीको तयारी। मद्दतको लागि, WHO आज एक नयाँ पहल सुरु, जेले देशहरूलाई भविष्यका महामारीहरूको लागि रामि तयारी गर्न मद्दत गर्ने। नयाँ पहल "तयारता र प्रतिक्रिया", PRET, समावेशे नवीनतम उपकरण र दृष्टिकोण, जे स्थापित भयो र COVID-19 महामारी र अन्य हालको सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य आपत्कालीन। यस पहल मार्फत, WHO ले देशहरूलाई महामारीको तयारीमा मद्दत गर्ने, किनकि धेरै क्षमता र क्षमताहरू साझा छन्। PRET, हेरफेर, समर्थन र एकीकृत तयारी र प्रतिक्रियालाई मद्दत गर्न, जस्तै विश्व स्वास्थ्य सभाको रिजोल्युसनहरूमा लेखिएको, हेरफेर। COVID-19 महामारी र अन्य स्वास्थ्य आपत्कालीनले देखा दिएको छ, देशहरूले संक्रामक रोगहरूको खतराहरूमा प्रतिक्रिया दिन तयार हुनु आवश्यक छ, जसमा अनुकूलित तयारी योजनाहरू छन् र कृषि जस्ता अन्य क्षेत्रहरूसँग रामि समन्वय र सहयोग। "तयारी, रोकथाम, र प्रतिक्रिया गतिविधिहरूले नसोमेश स्वास्थ्य क्षेत्रको मात्र जिम्मेवारी हुनुपर्छ," भन्यो डा. टेड्रोस एधानोम गेब्रेयसस, WHO निर्देशक-सामान्य। "जस्तै, स्वास्थ्य आपत्कालीनहरूले धेरै क्षेत्रहरूमा प्रभाव पार्छन्, त्यसैले हाम्रा तयारी र प्रतिक्रिया प्रयासहरूले पनि क्षेत्रहरू, विषयहरू र रोगहरू समावेश हुनुपर्छ। यो महत्वपूर्ण छ, समुदायको सहभागिता र समानताले हाम्रा प्रयासहरूमा केन्द्रित हुनुपर्छ, विशेष रूपमा ती जनसंख्याहरू, जे कमजोर र जोखिममा छन्।" PRET, WHO ले महामारीको तयारीमा, विशिष्ट रोगहरूमा ध्यान केन्द्रित नभई, मोड हेरफेर मार्फत, एक नयाँ विकास हो।

translations.source: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative. This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action
translations.reference: I-WHO izoqhubeka nokuthuthukisa nokusabalalisa imiyalelo ezifweni ezithile njengoba kudingeka. Ukwethulwa komzamo kuzokwenzeka ngosuku lokugcina lwemihlangano abangaphezu kuka-120 abamele Member State, ababambisani, noNobhala weWHO, ukuchaza indlela eya phambili yePRET okufaka ukuthuthuka kwebalazwe lokwenza lomhlaba kokuzilungiselela lobhubhane lwegciwane lokuphefumula. Imizamo yePRET isifundo sokuqala sigxile kumagciwane pokuphefumula, okufaka umkhuhlane, igciwane lekhorona namagciwane ezokuphefumula. Ngokunikezwa ubhubhane oluqhubekayo nokwesabisa okungenzeka emkhuhlaneni, lemojuli izokwenza amazwe abheke ngokucophelela, ukuhlola, ukubuyekeza imizamo yokuhlela ubhubhane lwezokuphefumula ukuqinisekisa banamandla okusebenzayo, namandla akhona. Uhlelo lusendleleni lokuhlonza iqoqo elilandelayo lamagciwane, njengama-arbovirus, ukuthi kubhekwane nawo ngaphansi kwalomzamo. Lokhu kuzolandela okuhamba phambili okuhlonziwe ngeziphakamiso eziyishumi zokuqinisa ukwakhiwa komhlaba kokuzilungiselela kokuphuthuma kwezempilo, ukuphendula nokubekezela (HEPR). Umzamo wePRET ukuyisa esikhathini esisha sokuzilungiselela kobhubhane nokumela uguquko lwemisebenzi ngqo yeWHO ukweseka wonke amaMember State ekuqiniseni ezempilo zokuzilungiselela okuphuthumayo, ukuvikela namandla okuphendula nokukwazi. Umzamo ungaphinde usize ukusebenzisa izinjongo nokuhlinzekwa kwePandemic Accord okwamanje okusaxoxwa ngayo yiWHO Member State. Kulandela lokho okufakiwe nezingxoxo emihlanganweni yababambe iqhaza, iWHO imema ababambisani kulesimemezelo somhlaba sokwenza ukuwandisa ukuzilungiselela kobhubhane legciwane lokuphefumula. Kufuneka ukwenza
translations.hypothesis: WHO जारी रहនឹង विकास र वितरणको मार्गदर्शन मा विशिष्ट रोगहरू जति आवश्यक छ। प्रक्षेपणको उद्घाटन गantungमा, धेरै 120 व्यक्तिहरू, जो सदस्य राज्यहरू, साझेदारहरू, र WHO सचिवालयका प्रतिनिधि हुन्, समावेश, यसले PRET को भविष्य मार्ग परिभाषित गर्ने, साथै श्वसन रोग महामारीको तयारीको लागि एक विश्वव्यापी कार्यान्वयन मार्ग विकास गर्ने। PRET पहलको पहिलो मोड्यमा ध्यान दिइएको छ, जसमा इन्फ्लुएन्जा, कोरोनावाइर र श्वसन सिन्साइटियल भाइरस समावेश छन्। COVID-19 महामारी र संभावित खतरा, जस्तै, च्यावको इन्फ्लुएन्जा, को देखते, यो मोड्यले देशहरूले आफ्नो श्वसन महामारी योजना प्रयासहरू आलोचनात्मक रूपमा समीक्षा गर्न, परीक्षण गर्न र अद्यतन गर्न मद्दत गर्ने, ताकि तिनीहरूले आवश्यक कार्यक्षमता र क्षमताहरूमा। एक प्रक्रिया चलिरहेको छ, ताकि अर्को समूहको रोगहरू, जस्तै, अर्बovirus, पहिचान गरिन्छ, जुन यस पहल अन्तर्गत समाधान गरिने। यो, दस प्रस्तावहरू मार्फत पहिचान गरिएका प्राथमिकताहरू, स्वास्थ्य आपत्कालीन तयारी, प्रतिक्रिया र लचीलापन (HEPR) को विश्वव्यापी संरचनालाई सुदृढ पार्ने, पछ्याउने। PRET पहलले महामारीको तयारीको लागि नयाँ युग ल्याउँछ, र WHO को मूल कार्यहरूलाई समर्थन गर्न, सदस्य राज्यहरूलाई स्वास्थ्य आपत्कालीन तयारी, रोकथाम र प्रतिक्रिया क्षमताहरू र क्षमताहरूलाई सुदृढ पार्ने। यो पहलले, Pandemic Accord, ले वर्तमानमा WHO सदस्य राज्यहरू द्वारा वार्ता गरिरहेको, उद्देश्य र प्रावधान कार्यान्वयन गर्न पनि मद्दत गर्न सक्छ। भेलामा सहभागी सदस्यहरूले दिने प्रतिक्रिया र चर्चाहरूलाई पछ्याएर, WHO, सदस्य राज्यहरूलाई श्वसन रोग महामारीको तयारीको लागि, यो विश्वव्यापी कार्य आह्वानमा संलग्न हुन आह्वान।

translations.source: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally. Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority
translations.reference: AmaMember State eWHO, nababambisani basebenza ndawonye ukuhlonza nokuhambisa phambili ukwenza izenzo zokuqinisa izwe, isifunda, nokuzilungiselela komhlaba kwezobhubhane nokuvela kwezifo ezithelelanayo ezisabisayo. Siyayibona indima yomphakathi nayo yonke imikhakha sidinga ukulinganisa nokugcina umfutho ekupheleni kwesakile yokusaba nokunganaki lokho kujwayelekile ekuzilungiseleni kobhubhane nokuphendula. Sikhumbula izifundo zobhubhane olwedlule ne-ephidemikhi, ikakhulukazi lezo ezidalwa amagciwane okuphefumula. Lawo magciwane azoqhubeka nokuletha ukusabisa komhlaba okukhulu ngamandla okudala izinkinga ezinkulu nokufa, izinhlelo zempilo ezingaphezu kwamandla, ukwehlisa ezomnotho zomhlaba, ukwengeza ukungalingani, okukhona ekungeneni kumathuluzi omabili okuvikela ubhubhane nokunakekelwa ezempilo kubona bonke abantu. Lesi yisimemezelo sokwenza ukuqhubeza ukuzilungiselela kobhubhane nokuvela kokwesaba emhlabeni. Ukusebenza kokuzilungiselela kuncike kubukhulu bokuhlela nokwenza kokuxhumana. Ukuzimisela kweqoqo kuzenze ezilandelayo kuzobonisa ukuqhubeka okutholakele ngoZibandlela 2025 futhi kuzochazwa (Ukuzilungiselela nokubekezela kokusabisayo okuvelayo (PRET) ukubheka umhlahlandlela: Ukulungiselelwa kokubuyekeziwe ukuhlela okuqinisekisa izenzo zokuhamba phambili nokucabanga izifundo zezigemegeme ezedlule. Ukubona ingcuphe efakwe amagciwane ezokuphefumula, ukuhlela kobhubhane lamagciwane ezokuphefumula ancike kuzingqikithi ezihlonzwe kusifundo sePRET #1: Ukuhlelela ubhubhane lwamagciwane ezokuphefumula kuhamba phambili
translations.hypothesis: WHO, सदस्य राज्यहरू, र साझेदारहरू काम सम्बद्धताको लागि, सिर्जना र कार्यान्वयन गर्न प्राथमिकतायुक्त कार्यहरू, ताकि राष्ट्रिय, प्रादेशिक, र सवैव्यापी तयारीलाई महामारी र उभरिरहेको संक्रामक रोगहरूको खतरालाई बढावा दिन। हामीले समुदाय र सबै क्षेत्रहरूको भूमिका, र सुसंगत र स्थिर गतिशीलताको आवश्यकतालाई पहिचान गरेका छौँ, ताकि महामारीको तयारी र प्रतिक्रियामा व्याप्त, जुन धेरै सामान्य छ, यस चक्र समाप्त हुन सकू। हामी विगतको महामारी र महामारीबाट सिकेका पाठहरू सम्झन आवश्यक छ, विशेष गरी श्वसन रोगहरूले निम्त्याएका। यी रोगहरूले, र भविष्यमा पनि, एक महत्त्वपूर्ण सवैव्यापी खतरा प्रस्तुत गर्ने, र असाधारण रोग र मृत्युको कारण बन्ने, स्वास्थ्य प्रणालीलाई कमजोर पार्ने, सवैव्यापी अर्थतन्त्रलाई अस्थिर पार्ने, र असमानतालाई बढावा दिने, जुन महामारी रोकथामका उपकरण र सबै मानिसहरूका लागि स्वास्थ्य सेवामा पहुँचमा विद्यमान छ। यो एक आह्वान हो, ताकि सवैव्यापी रूपमा महामारी र उभरिरहेको खतराहरूको तयारीलाई थ्रस्ट गर्न। प्रभावकारी तयारीमा ठोस योजना र समन्वयित कार्य आवश्यक छ। निम्न कार्यहरूमा सामूहिक प्रतिबद्धताले, 2025 नोभेम्बरसम्म प्रगति हासिल गर्न मद्दत गर्ने, जस्तै (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) निगरानी ढाँचामा वर्णन गरिएको छ: तयारीका योजनाहरू अद्यतन गर्नुहोस्, जसले प्राथमिकतायुक्त कार्यहरूलाई पुष्टि गर्छ र विगतको घटनाहरूबाट सिकेका पाठहरूलाई ध्यानमा राख्छ। श्वसन रोगहरूले निम्त्याउने जोखिमलाई पहिचान गरेकोले, PRET Module #1 मा पहिचान गरिएका विषयहरूमा आधारित श्वसन रोग महामारीको तयारी, एक प्राथमिकता हो।

translations.source: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding. At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.reference: Ukunyusa ukuxhumana kubabambisani ekuzilungiseleni kobhubhane ukuhlela ngezinhlelo zokuxhumana nokusebenzisana. Lokhu kufaka ukwakha izinhlelo ezilinganayo ukwenza imisebenzi ngokuhlanganyela; Nokwabelana ngolwazi ekwenzeni okuhle, izingqinamba kanye namathuba. Ukunikela ngotshalomali olugcinekile, izimali nokubheka kokulungela ubhubhane ngokugxila ekubhekeni izikhala ezihlonze ngesikhathi sobhubhane olwedlule. Ubhubhane lweCOVID-19 luphendule ukukhombisa ukuthi yikuphi okungazuzwa ngokuzibophezela kwezepolitiki, ukukhuluma komphakathi kanye noxhasomali. Phakathi nalomsebenzi ukuqinisekisa ukulingana ukulungela obhubhane olulandelayo ndawonye. Ukwenza komphakathi wonke kuyadingeka ukwenza inqubekela phambili evezwe kule simemezelo sokwenza. Ukwenza kumele kuqinise imiphakathi ebekezelayo; egcinayo nokugcineka nokwakha izindlela zezinhlelo; nokubambisa kwamandla amakhulu kokuzilungiselela okuphuthumayo nokuphendula. Lesi simemezelo sokwenza sakhishwa eKuzilungiseleni nokubekezela kokuvela kokusabisayo (PRET): Umhlangano womhlaba wekusasa lobhubhane lwamagciwane ezokuphefumula obanjwe ngo-24-26 Mbasa 2023 eGeneva, eSwitzerland.
translations.hypothesis: बढावा दिगोता मध्यस्थबीच, मार्फत व्यवस्थित समन्वय र सहयोग। यस समावेशे, निर्माण न्यायपूर्ण प्रणाली; संयुक्त अभ्यास; र जानकारी साझा गर्ने, उत्तम अभ्यास, चुनौती, र अवसरहरू। समर्पित लगानी, वित्तपोषण, र निगरानी, मार्फत महामारी तयारी, जसमा विशेष ध्यान दिइएको छ, समाधान गर्ने विगतको महामारी र प्रकोपहरूले पहिलेज। प्रतिक्रियाको COVID-19ले हेरफेर, समुदायको सहभागिता, र वित्तपोषणले हासिल गर्न सकस। यो कार्यको मूल उद्देश्यले, तयार हुनु, ताकि अर्को महामारीमा, हामी सबै एकसाथ तयार हुन सक्छौं। सम्पूर्ण समाजको कार्य आवश्यक छ, ताकि क्रमिक रूपमा हेरफेर गरिएको कार्य पूर्ण हुन सकस। कार्यान्वयनले, समुदायको लचीलापन बढावा; नियमित प्रणाली, कायम राखे, र विकसित; र आपत्कालीन तयारी र प्रतिक्रियाको विस्तृत क्षमता प्रयोग। यो कार्य आह्वान, जारी गरिएको हो, "तयारी र लचीलापन, उभरिरहेको खतराहरू" (PRET): भविष्यका श्वसन रोग महामारीको लागि, 24-26 एप्रिल 2023, जेनेभा, स्विट्जरल्याण्ड।

translations.source: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year. “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.reference: I-WHO inxenxa ukunyuswa kokwenza ngamathuluzi aphakanyisiwe ukulwa nomalaleveva Ukumaka uSuku lukaMalaleveva eMhlabeni, Inhlangano Yezempilo Yomhlaba (WHO) izokhipha isimememzelo sokunyusa ukwenza kokungenelela okukhona nokusha ukusindisa izimpilo kumalaleveva. Okungafika kuzigidi ezingu-1.5 izingane ezisengcupheni enkulu yokugula nokufa okusuka kumalaleveva eGhana, eKenya naseMalawi manje sebethole umgomo wokuqala kamalaleveva, RTS, S/AS01 (RTS,S), siyabonga kuhlelo lokuhlola oluqhubekayo oluxhunyaniswa yiWHO. Ukuhlolwa komgomo wamalaleveva, owethulwe ngo-2019,kwenyusa ukulingana kokuthola ukuvikelwa kumalaleveva kulabo ababuthaka kakhulu futhi kusindisa izimpilo. Ukwenziwe kakhulu, iWHO ilinganisela ukuthi umgomo kamalaleveva ungasingisa izimpilo eziyizinkulungwane eziyishumi zezingane ngonyaka. "Sinamathuluzi ukwehlisa umalaleveva,iphekheji yokungenenela ebandakanya ukulawula ivektha,imithi yokuvikela, ukuhlola nokwelapha," kusho uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO. "Lokhu kulandelwa ngumgomo ophephile nosebenzayo kamalaleveva, ongasindisa izimpilo eziyizinkulungwane eziyishumi zezingane unyaka ngamunye. Ngotshalomali olugcinekile nokunyuka kwemizamo ukufika kulabo abasengcupheni kakhulu, ukuqedwa kwamalaleveva emazweni amaningi sekuyafika. "Usuku lukaMalaleveva eMhlabeni 2023 lumakwe ngaphansi kwengqikithi, "Isikhathi sokuwqeda umalaleveva, tshala, yenza kabusha yenza". Kule ngqikithi, iWHO inxenxa okunye ukwenza okusebenzayo kwamathuluzi akhona namasu okuvikela, ukuxilonga nokwelapha umalaleveva, ikakhulukazi kulesosibalo esingcindezelekile.
translations.hypothesis: WHO आह्वानको छ कि थप उपकरणहरू प्रयोग गरिन ताकि मलेरिया लडिएको जा,marking विश्व मलेरिया दिवस, सव विश्व स्वास्थ्य सङ्गठन (WHO) ले नया र existentes हस्तक्षेपहरूको थप कार्यान्वयनको आह्वान गरिरहेको छ, ताकि जीवनलाई मलेरियाबाट जोगाउन सको. लगभग 1.5 मिलियन बच्चाहरू, जेसमा मलेरियाबाट बिरामी हुनु वा मर्न जोखिम छ, मा, Ghana, केन्या र मलावी, अब उनीहरूले पहिलो मात्राको मलेरिया खोप, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), प्राप्त गरेका छन्, धन्यवादलाई WHO द्वारा सञ्चालित एक पायलट कार्यक्रम। मलेरिया खोप पायलट, जेला 2019 मा सुरु गरिएको, सबैभन्दा कमजोर समूहका लागि मलेरिया रोकथामको पहुँचमा समानता बढाउँदै, र जीवन बचाउँदै। यदि व्यापक रूपमा लागू गरिएको छ, WHO ले भविष्यवाणी गरेको छ कि मलेरिया खोपहरू हरेक वर्ष हजारौं बच्चाहरूको जीवन बचाउन सक्छ। "हामीसँग मलेरिया लडने उपकरणहरू छन्, एक प्याकेज, जेला कीट नियन्त्रण, निवारक औषधी, परीक्षण र उपचार समावेश छन्," भन्यो WHO निर्देशक-सामान्य, डा. टेड्रोस अदहनोम गेब्रेयसस। "यी, साथै एक सुरक्षित र प्रभावकारी मलेरिया खोप, जेला हरेक वर्ष हजारौं बच्चाहरूको जीवन बचाउन सक्छ। स्थिर लगानी र सबैभन्दा जोखिममा रहेका समूहहरू पुगने प्रयासहरूमा, मलेरियाको उन्मूलन धेरै देशहरूमा हासिल गर्न योग्य छ।" "विश्व मलेरिया दिवस 2023, "समय छ: शून्य मलेरिया, लगानी, नवोन्मेष, कार्यान्वयन" विषय अन्तर्गत मनाइँदै। यस विषय अन्तर्गत, WHO थप प्रभावकारी उपकरण र रणनीतिहरूको कार्यान्वयनको आह्वान गरिरहेको छ, जेला मलेरिया रोकथाम, निदान र उपचार, विशेष रूपमा कमजोर जनसंख्यामा।

translations.source: According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations. Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments;
translations.reference: Ngokombiko wokugcina wamalaleveva emhlabeni,oshicilelwe ngoZibandlela 2022, bekunesilinganiso sika-247 wezigidi zamacala amasha kamalaleveva ngo-2021. I-WHO African Region iyaqhubeka ukuthwala umthwalo onzima wesifo- okuballelwa esilinganiswenisa ku-95% wamacala kamalaleveva (u-234 wezigidi) no-96% wokufa (593 000) ngo-2021. Kungafika ku-80% ukufa okudalwa umalaleveva eSifundeni se-Afrika obukukubantwana abangaphansi kweminyaka emihlanu. Amasu amasha namathuluzi Amazweasenze inqubekela phambili ekwandiseni ukungena kumisebenzi yamalaleveva kusibalo sabantu esisengcupheni kakhulu. Ngaphandle kokuqhubeka abantu abaningi abasengcupheni yamalaleveva basantula ukungena emisebenzini engavimba, noma itthole bese yelapha isifo. Izingqinamba ekwandeni kokungena kumisebenzi kamalaleveva sezihlanganisisiwe,ikakhulukazi esu-Saharan Afrika, ngobhubhane lweCOVID-19, ukujika izinkinga zobuntu, uxhasomali olunezivimbo, izinhlelo zokubheka ezintekenteke, ukwehla ekwenzeni kwamathuluzi okulwa ngqo nomalaleveva. Ukubhekana nalokhu okusabisayo nokweseka amazwe ekwakheni izinhlelo zokubekezela zamalaleveva, iWHO isanda kushicilela amasu amasha nomhlahlandlela: isu elisha lelinesidakamizwa esingenamalaleveva ukuvika eAfrika; umzamo omusha wokumisa ukusabalala kweAnophelesi stephensi ezindaweni zasemadolobheni;
translations.hypothesis: भेर अनुसार, नवीनतम विश्व रिपोर्ट, जी प्रकाशित भेल मा 2022, अनुमानित 247 मिलियन नया मामिलाहरू अफ मलेरियामा 2021। WHO अफ्रिका क्षेत्र लगातार ले यो रोगको भारी भार – जो अनुमानित 95% सबै मलेरिया मामिला (234 मिलियन) र 96% सबै मृत्यु (593,000) मा योगदान, 2021। लगभग 80% मलेरिया मृत्युमा अफ्रिका क्षेत्रमा, थिए बच्चाहरू जो पाँच वर्ष मुन। नयाँ रणनीति र उपकरण देशहरूले, अधिकांश जोखिम समूहहरूका लागि मलेरिया सेवाहरू पहुँच विस्तार गर्न, केही प्रगति हासिल गरेका छन्। यद्यपि, केही प्रगति भए तापनि, धेरै मानिसहरू जो मलेरियाको उच्च जोखिममा, अझैले सेवाहरू अभाव छन् जसले रोगलाई रोकथाम, पहिचान र उपचार गर्न सक्छ। मलेरिया सेवाहरू पहुँच विस्तार गर्ने चुनौतीहरू, विशेष रूपमा सब-सहारन अफ्रिका, COVID-19 महामारी, converging मानवीय संकट, सीमित स्रोत, कमजोर निगरानी प्रणाली, र मूल मलेरिया-लड़ाई उपकरणहरूको प्रभावशीलता घटोत् घट, थप जटिल बनेको छ। यस खतराहरूलाई समाधान गर्न र देशहरूलाई थप लचीला मलेरिया कार्यक्रमहरू निर्माण गर्न मद्दत गर्न, WHO हालै नयाँ रणनीति र ढाँचाहरू प्रकाशित गरेको छ, समावेश: अफ्रिकामा प्रति-मलेरिया औषधिको प्रतिरोध नियन्त्रणको लागि नयाँ रणनीति; शहर वातावरणमा Anopheles stephensi फैलावट रोक्ने लागि नयाँ उपक्रम;

translations.source: a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact. Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024.
translations.reference: umhlahlandlela omusha, uthuthukiswe ndawonye yiWHO neUN-Habitat, ukuyalela babaholi bedolobha ekulawulweni kwemalalaeveva edolobheni. ithuluzi elisha ukusiza amazwe ahlola umalaleveva wabio wezinhlelo zokubheka nokuhlonza izindawo zotshalomali I-WHO iphinde yanyusa ukwethembeka, ukubalula nokuthola iziphakamiso zemalaleveva. Ukuhlanganiswa kwemiyalelo yeWHO kwamalaleveva sekukhona manje ngezinkundla ezimbili zedijithali: iMAGICapp kanye ne-"MalariaToolkit" isisetshenziswa. I-WHO igqugquzela amazwe ukuthi ahlanganise kuziphakamiso ekuhlelweni kwezifo zendawo kumthelela omkhulu. Izindlela zokungenelela okusha Ukuqhubeka kotshalomali ekuthuthukisweni nokubekwa kwemigomo emisha kamalaleveva namathuluzi esizukulwane esilandelayo kuzobalulekaukuthola okuqondwe ngomalaleveva emhlabeni 2030. Umgomo wesibili kamalaleveva, umgomo iR21/Matrix-M (R21), uma ugunyaziwe ungasiza ukuvala isikhala esikalekayo phakathi kwesaplayi mokufuneka nokuqhubeka ukwehlisa ukugula kwezingane nokufa okudalwa umalaleveva. Kuhamba phambili kuWHO ukuqhubeka ngokubheka kangoti kweR21 malaleveva ngesikhathi sekwengezwe okuphepha okubalulekile,nemininingo kusuka ekuqhubekeni kukaR21 isigaba 3 sesikhona nokinikezelwa kuWHO. Isigaba sokukhiqiza iRTS,S umgomo wamalaleveva emazweni engeziwe eAfrika kulindeleke ukuthi aqale ngokuqala kuka-2024.
translations.hypothesis: एक नया ढाँचा, विकसितकोय संयुक्तले WHO र UN-Habitat, मार्फत, जसले शहरका नेताहरूलाई शहरमा मलेरिया नियन्त्रणमा मार्गदर्शन गर्ने। एक नयाँ उपकरण, मार्फत देशहरू सक्छन आफ्नो मलेरिया निगरानी प्रणाली मूल्याङ्कन गर्न र क्षेत्रहरू पहिचान गर्न। WHO ले आफ्नो मलेरिया सिफारिसहरूमा पारदर्शिता, लचिलो र पहुँच पनि बढाएको छ। एकीकृत WHO दिशानिर्देशहरूको लागि मलेरिया अब उपलब्ध छन् मार्फत दुई डिजिटल प्लेटफर्म: MAGICapp र "Malaria Toolkit" एप। WHO प्रोत्साहित गर्छ देशहरू सिफारिसहरूलाई स्थानीय रोग अवस्थाअनुकूल बनाउन, ताकि अधिकतम प्रभाव हासिल भइ स। सम्भावित नयाँ हस्तक्षेपहरू। नयाँ मलेरिया खोप र अर्को पुतर्का उपकरणहरूको विकास र कार्यान्वयनमा लगातार लगानी, महत्वपूर्ण छ, ताकि २०३० मावै विश्वव्यापी मलेरिया लक्ष्य हासिल भइ स। दोस्रो मलेरिया खोप, R21/Matrix-M (R21) खोप, यदि स्वीकृत, सक्या सक्छ, महत्त्वपूर्ण अन्तरलाई भरपारी र मलेरियाबाट बच्चाहरूको बिराएर र मृत्युलाई थप कम गर्न। WHO ले R21 मलेरिया खोपको विस्तृत र कुशल विशेषज्ञ मूल्याङ्कन जारी राख्न, प्राथमिकता छ, एकपटक थप महत्त्वपूर्ण सुरक्षा र प्रभावकारिता डेटा उपलब्ध छन् र WHO लाई प्रदान गरिएको छ। RTS,S मलेरिया खोपको चरणबद्ध परिचय, थप देशहरूमा, अफ्रिकामा, २०२४ मा सुरु हुन अपेक्षित छ।

translations.source: In the vector control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. Tools under evaluation include, for example, new types of insecticide-treated nets, targeted baits that attract mosquitoes, spatial repellents, lethal house lures (eaves tubes) and genetic engineering of mosquitoes. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT treatments in the field of antimalarial medicines due to the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. The development of next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," which use a combination of artemisinin and two partner drugs, is underway to reduce the risk of drug resistance. Notes to the editor:For more information on the WHO World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.reference: Endaweni yokulawula ivekitha,kukhona imikhiqizoemisha engu-28 kokuzayo kweR&D. Amathuluzi asabhekisiswa afka, isibonelo, izinhlobo ezintsha zamanethi okulapha izinambuzane, izicuphomziqonde ukudonsa omiyane, isipeshali ripheleli, okwasendlini, (i-eaves tubes) nobunjiniyela bofuzo lwemiyane. Abacwaningi bahambisa phambili ukuthuthuka kokulashwa okungeyona i-ACTS emikhakheni wemithi omele umalaleveva ngenxa yokuphuthuma nokusabalala kokungavuminokuncane kwe-artemisinin. Ukdevelophwa kwemithi yesizukulwane esilandelayo, njenge ACTS, okusebenzisa ukuhlanganiswa kwe-artemisinin nezidakamizwa zimbili, kusendleleni kuzokwehlisa ingcuphe yokungavumi kwesidakamizwa. Amanothi aya kumhleli: ngolwazi olwengeziwe ekukhankaseni ngoSuku lukamalaleveva eMhlabeni lweWHO, vakashela u-http://www.who.int/campains/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.hypothesis: यद्यपि, कतिय नियन्त्रण, थयerdapat 28 उत्पादनस नयाँ उत्पादनस कतिय नियन्त्रण क्षेत्रमा। उपकरणस जुन मूल्याङ्कन गरिदै छन्, समावेश छन्, उदाहरणका रूपमा, नयाँ प्रकारका कीटनाशक-उपचारित जाली, लक्षित चारा जसले डासहरूलाई आकर्षित, स्थान-आधारित निवारक, घातक घर आकर्षण (एवज ट्यूब), र डासहरूको आनुवंशिक अभियन्त्रण। अनुसन्धानकर्ताहरू, मलेरिया विरोधी औषधिको क्षेत्रमा, गैर-एसीटी उपचारको विकासलाई प्राथमिकता दिन्छन्, किनकि आंशिक आर्टेमिसिन प्रतिरोधको उद्भव र फैलावट। "ट्रिपल एसीटी" जस्ता, जेनेरेशन औषधिको विकास, जेला आर्टेमिसिन र दुई साझेदार औषधिको मिश्रण प्रयोग, औषधिको प्रतिरोधको जोखिम घटाउनको लागि, अद्यावधि। नोटहरू: थप जानकारीको लागि, WHO विश्व मलेरिया दिवस अभियान, कृपया भेट्नुहोस्: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023

translations.source: mRNA Technology Transfer Programme moves to the next phase of its development Inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen, South Africa. Partners gather in Cape Town to discuss the next phase of the mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. Over 200 international participants working with the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme met in Cape Town this week for their first face-to-face meeting. Joined by World Health Organization (WHO) Director General, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and Dr. Joe Phaahla, Minister of Health and Minister of Trade and Industry, Mr. Ebrahim Patel of South Africa together with high-level officials from funding countries, this unique meeting reviewed the progress since WHO and Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) launched it in June 2021. “I am delighted to be here in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for mRNA technology transfer to give low- and middle-income countries equitable access to vaccines and other lifesaving health products,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “I am immensely proud of the achievement of all those involved in this project; in less than two years we have shown that when we work collaboratively, we succeed collectively.” The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners from 15 countries in the Programme, leading experts, industry, civil society representatives, and funders. During the five-day meeting, participants will share progress and discuss critical enablers for the sustainability of the Programme such as intellectual property issues and regulatory aspects, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and key applications relevant to LMICs in other disease areas such as HIV and tuberculosis. The COVID-19 pandemic has underscored that gross inequity exists in access to health products, especially vaccines.
translations.reference: Uhlelo loKudlulisela ezobuchwepheshe i-mRNA iya esigabeni esilandelayo ekuthuthukeni kwayo Ukwethulwa kwehabhu yezobuchwepheshe imRNA indawo e-Afrigen, eNingizimu Afrika. Ababambisani bahlangane eKapa ukuxoxa ngesigaba esilandelayo semRNA umgomo ngokuthuthukisa nokwabelana ngolwazi. Ngaphezu kwababambiqhaza abangu-200 bamazwe angaphandle abasebenza ngoHlelo loKudlulisela lezoBuchwepheshe beMRNA abahlangane eKapa kulelisonto ukuze bahlangane bebonana. Ukujoyinwa iNhlangano yeZempilo eMhlabeni (WHO) uMqondisi Jikelele uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus noDkt Joe Phaahla uNgqongqoshe weZempilo noNgqongqoshe Wezimboni nokuhweba uMnumzane Ebrahim waseNingizimu Afrika ndawonye nezikhulu ezisezingeni eliphezulu eziphuma emazweni axhasile, lomhlangano owehlukile ubheke ukuqhubeka kusukela ukwethula kweWHO neMedicines Patent Pool (MPP) ngoNhlangulana 2021. "Ngiyajabula ukuba lapha eKapa kanye nababambisani bethu ukuzokweseka imodeli egcinekayo yokudluliselwa kobuchwepheshe mRNA ukunikeza amazwe anemali engenayo encane kuya kwephakathi nendawo ukuthola ngokulingana umgomo kanye neminye imikhiqizo yezempilo esindisa izimpilo zabantu," kusho uMqondisi Jikelele weWHO, uDkt Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. "Ngijabule kakhulu ngokuzuziwe kubona bonke ababambe iqhaza kulephrojekthi,ngaphansi kweminyaka emibili sesikhombisile ukuthi uma sisebenza ngokubambisana, siphumelela sonke." Ababambiqhaza bomhlangano kufaka ababambisani bomkhiqizo wasemazweni angu-15 oHlelweni, ongoti abaphambili, imboni abameli bomphakathi kanye nabaxhasimali. Ngesikhathi somhlangano wezinsuku esinhlanu, ababambiqhaza bazokwabelana ngokuqhubeka baxoxe ngokubucayi kwezinto zokugcineka koHlelo njengezinkinga zegunyabunikazi nezindlela zokulawula kanyenesayensi yezobuchwepheshe be-mRNA nokusebenza okubalulekile okuhlobene neLMIC kwezinye izndawo zezifo njengeHIV nesifo sofuba. Ubhubhane lweCOVID-19 sekukale ngaphansi ukuthi ukungalingani okuphelele kukhona ekutholeni imikhiqizo yezempilo, ikakhulukazi imigomo.
translations.hypothesis: कार्यक्रम "स्थानान्तरण प्रविधि mRNA" सर्दै, कति विकास। उदघाटन केन्द्र "mRNA" प्रविधि, Afrigen, दक्षिण अफ़्रीका। साझेदारहरू भेटीमा, Cape Town, ताकि छलफल गरून्, कति विकास, र जानकारी साझा गरून्। धेरै २००, अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय प्रतिभा, जो "प्रविधि mRNA" प्रविधि, सर्दै, भेटीमा, Cape Town, यस हप्ता, आफ्नो पहिलो भेट। सामेल, निर्देशक जेनेरल, विश्व स्वास्थ्य सङ्गठन (WHO), डा. टेड्रोस एधानोम गेब्रेयसस, र डा. जो फाहला, मन्त्री स्वास्थ्य, र मन्त्री वाणिज्य र उद्योग, श्री एब्राहम पेटेल, दक्षिण अफ़्रीका, साथै, उच्च-स्तरीय अधिकारीहरू, कति देशहरू, यो अनौपचारिक भेटी, समीक्षाहरू, कति विकास, यतिल्यै, WHO र Medicines Patent Pool (MPP), यसमा, जून 2021। "म खुसी, म यहाँ, Cape Town, संग, साझेदारहरू, समर्थन, एक स्थायी मोडेल, "प्रविधि mRNA", ताकि निम्न- र मध्यम-आय, देशहरू, समान पहुँच, खोप, र अन्य जीवन रक्षक, स्वास्थ्य उत्पाद," भन्यो, निर्देशक जेनेरल, WHO, डा. टेड्रोस एधानोम गेब्रेयसस। "म, धेरै गर्व, यस परियोजना, सबै, जो संलग्न; कम, दो वर्ष, म, हेरफेर, सामूहिक।" प्रतिभा, समावेश, साझेदारहरू, 15 देशहरू, "प्रविधि", विशेषज्ञ, उद्योग, सङ्गठन, र, दाताहरू। यस भेटी, पाँच दिन, प्रतिभा, साझा, प्रगति, र छलफल, महत्वपूर्ण, "प्रविधि", जस्तै, बौद्धिक सम्पदा, मुद्दाहरू, र, नियम, तथा, विज्ञान, "प्रविधि", र, मुख्य, अनुप्रयोगहरू, जस्तै, HIV, र, क्षयरोग। COVID-19, महामारी, स्पष्ट, असमानता, स्वास्थ्य उत्पाद, विशेष, खोप।

translations.source: As of March 2023, more than three years after WHO declared COVID-19 a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC), 69.7% of the global population had received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine. Notably, this proportion still remains below 30% in low-income countries (LICs) . This Programme aims to contribute to equitable access to mRNA vaccines by increasing the distribution of sustainable manufacturing capacity across LMICs, enhancing regional and inter-regional collaborations, and developing and empowering a local workforce through tailored and inclusive training and expert support. What is unique in the mRNA Technology Transfer model is the multilateral process that allows sharing of technologies to multiple recipients so that through local and regional production those in need can be reached rapidly. Dr Phaahla, Minister of Health South Africa, said: “What we see here today, is a moment in history, a Programme that is aimed at empowering LMICs through a global collaborative network. I am thrilled to see the progress made in such a relatively short time and welcome the support from so many different countries - countries like South Africa that have a strong vibrant biomanufacturing capacity and that are willing to work together, learn from and share with each other.” Minister Blade Nzimande of Higher Education and Science, said: “Ours is the vision of the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub beyond just COVID-19. The capabilities we are building are looking to empower us to deal with other future pandemics whose vaccines could use the same mRNA technology platform. It is a vision that has set its eyes on diseases that are prevalent in our environments such as Malaria, Tuberculosis and HIV and AIDS.” This occasion also marks an important milestone for the Programme with the inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen in Cape Town in the presence of Dr. Tedros and Dr. Matshidiso Moeti WHO Regional Director for Africa, Charles Gore Executive Director of MPP and the Honorable Ministers who took part in a ribbon cutting ceremony at Afrigen.
translations.reference: Kusukela ngoNdasa 2023, ngaphezu kweminyaka emithathu ngemva kokuthi Iwho imemezele iCOVID-19 njengePublic Health of International Concern (PHEIC), u-69.7% wesibalo sabantu emhlabeni sebethole okungenani idosi eyodwa yomgomo weCOVID-19. Okuqaphelekayo lengxenye izohlala ingaphansi kuka-30% emazweni anemali engenayo encane (amaLIC). Loli Hlelo luhlose ukuxhasa ekutholeno okulinganayo emigomweni ye-mRNA ngokunyusa ukuhambisa kwamandla okukhiqiza okugcinekayo kuwona wonke amaLMIC, ukwandisa okwesifunda nokubambisana kwaphakathi nezifunda nokudevelopha nokusiza ukusebenza kwasendaweni ngokwenziwa kokuqeqeshwa nokwesekwa ngongoti. Yikuphi okwehlukile kumodeli yoKudluliselwa kweZobuchwepheshe kwe-mRNA kuhlelo olunezinto eziningi oluvumela ukwabelana ngobuchwepheshe,ukuthola okuningi ukuze ukukhiqizwa ngokwesifunda nendawo kulabo abadingayo kungafinyelela ngokuphindiwe. UDkt Phaahla, uNgqongqoshe weZempilo eNingizimu Afrika, uthe: "Esikubona namhlanje lapha,umzumzu womlando, uHlelo oluhlose ukusiza iLMIC ngohlelokuxhumana lokubambisana emhlabeni. Ngiyajabula ukubona inqubekela phambili eyenziwe esikhathini esifishane kangaka, siyakwamukela ukwesekwa okusuka emazweni amaningi ahlukene-amazwe afana neNingizimu Afrika anokukhiqizwa okuqinile anamandla, futhi sizimisele ukusebenza ndawonye, ukufunda nokwabelana kuthina sonke." Ungqongqoshe Blade Nzimande weMfundo ePhakeme neSayensi, uthe: "Umbono wethu weHabhu yoKudlulisela ubuchwepheshe be-mRNA kungaphezu kweCOVID-19 nje. Amandla okuthi siyakha afuna usizo kuthina ukubhekana nobhubhane lwesikhathi esizayo imigomo yalo engasebenzisa inkundla efanayo yobuchwepheshe be-mRNA. Kungumbono osubeke amehlo awo ekubhekaneni nezifo ezivamile ezindaweni zethu njengoMalaleveva, isifo sofuba, iHIV ne-AIDS." Lomcimbi uphinde umake uhambo olubalulekile loHlelo nokwethulwa kwendawo yeHabhu yobuchwepheshe ne-mRNA e-Afrigen eKapa kukhona uDkt Tedros noDkt Matshidiso Moeti weWHO Regional Director for Afrika, Charles Gore uMqondisi oMkhulu weMPP kanye noNgqongqoshe abahlonishwayo ababamba iqhaza ekusikweni kweribhini emcimbini e-Afrigen.
translations.hypothesis: यद्यपि, डिसेम्बर 2023, धेरै तीन वर्षयताय, WHOले COVID-19 लाई अन्तर्राष्ट्रिय चिंतातான सार्वजनिक स्वास्थ्य आपत्स्य घोषित गरेको, 69.7% विश्व जनसंख्याले कम्तिमा एक डोसको COVID-19 खोप प्राप्त गरेका थिए। विशेष रूपमा, यो अनुपात, निम्न-आय मुलुकहरू (LICs) मा, 30% भन्दा तल छ। यो कार्यक्रम, स्थायी उत्पादन क्षमतालाई LMICs मा बढावा दिन, प्रादेशिक र क्षेत्र-बीचको सहयोगलाई सुदृढ पार्न, र अनुकूल र समावेशी तालिम र विशेषज्ञ समर्थन मार्फत स्थानीय कार्यबल विकास गर्न, mRNA खोपहरुको समान पहुँचमा योगदान पुग्ने लक्ष्य राखेको छ। mRNA प्रविधि स्थानान्तरण मोडेलमा, एउटा बहुपक्षीय प्रक्रिया छ, जेले विभिन्न प्राप्तकर्ताहरूलाई प्रविधि साझा गर्न अनुमति दिन्छ, ताकि स्थानीय र क्षेत्र उत्पादन मार्फत, जसलाई आवश्यकता छ, उनीहरूलाई चाँडै पुग्न सकिन्छ। डा. फाहाला, दक्षिण अफ्रिकाका स्वास्थ्य मन्त्रीले भने: "यसले हामी आज देख्छ, एउटा ऐतिहासिक क्षण हो, एउटा कार्यक्रम जसले LMICs लाई विश्वव्यापी सहयोगात्मक नेटवर्क मार्फत सशक्त बनाउने लक्ष्य राखेको छ। म, अपेक्षाकृत छोटो समयमा हासिल गरिएको प्रगति हेर्न र धेरै विभिन्न देशहरूबाट समर्थन प्राप्त गर्न उत्साहित छु - दक्षिण अफ्रिका जस्ता देशहरू, जससँग मजबूत र जीवन्त बायो-उत्पादन क्षमता छ, र जुन एकअर्का साथ काम गर्न, सिक्न र साझा गर्न इच्छुक छन्।" उच्च शिक्षा र विज्ञानका मन्त्री, ब्लेड निमandeले भने: "हाम्रो दृष्टिकोण, mRNA प्रविधि स्थानान्तरण केन्द्र, केवल COVID-19 भन्दा माथि छ। हामी निर्माण गरिरहेको क्षमताहरू, हामीलाई भविष्यका महामारीहरूसँग सामना गर्न सक्षम बनाउने लक्ष्य राखेको छ, जसका खोपहरु समान mRNA प्रविधि प्लेटफर्म प्रयोग गर्न सक्छन्। यो एउटा दृष्टिकोण हो, जेले हाम्रो वातावरणमा व्याप्त रोगहरू जस्तै मलेरिया, क्षयरोग र एचआईभी/एड्स मा ध्यान केन्द्रित गरेको छ।" यो अवसर, कार्यक्रमका लागि एउटा महत्त्वपूर्ण क्षण पनि हो, जहाँ Afrigen मा Cape Town मा, डा. टेड्रोस र डा. मातिसिडो मोएटी, WHO को अफ्रिकाका क्षेत्रीय निर्देशक, चार्ल्स गोर, MPP को कार्यकारी निर्देशक, र सम्मानजनक मन्त्रीहरूले, Afrigen मा रिबन काट्ने समारोहमा भाग लिन, mRNA प्रविधि केन्द्रको उद्घाटन गरिएको छ।

translations.source: Professor Petro Terblanche, Executive Director of Afrigen said: “The entire Afrigen team are thrilled to reach this important milestone with the completion of the mRNA technology platform. This platform is housed within the end-to-end mRNA vaccine development and production facility where the mRNA Hub Covid 19 vaccine candidate AfriVac 2121 is currently in scale-up phase. Over the last 18 months, Afrigen has undergone an incredible transformation with the support of a network of partners and mentors enabled by this Programme. We have grown our capability and capacity to meet the highest quality standards of mRNA vaccine development, serving the objective to build sustainable capacity in LMICs to produce mRNA vaccines”. Afrigen has successfully established a COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing process at a laboratory scale and is currently scaling up that process to a level suitable for manufacturing vaccine batches to be used in Phase I/ II clinical trials to GMP standards. In a parallel process, Afrigen will continue to carry out training and technology transfer to the network partners. The funders play a crucial role in supporting the Programme with total funding to date at US$ 117 million and France being the first to fund the mRNA technology transfer work. Present at the meeting were representatives from the European Commission, Belgium, Germany, as well as Norway, Canada, African Union, South Africa, and the ELMA Foundation. Caroline Delany, GlobalAffairs Canada’s Director General for Southern and Eastern Africa said: “Canada reaffirms its continued support of the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme and we are delighted to announce a further contribution of 15 million Canadian dollars at the meeting today. This brings Canada’s total contribution to the Programme up to 45 million Canadian dollars.
translations.reference: USolwazi Petro Terblance, uMqondisi oMkhulu we-Afrigen, uthe: "Iqembu lonke le-Afrigen liyajabula ukufika kulokhu okubalulekile nokuqedwa kwenkundla yezobuchwepheshe be-mRNA. Lenkundla inendawo ekupheleni kokuthuthukiswa komgomo mRNA nokukhiqizwa kwendawo lapho ihabhu ye-mRNA yomgomo weCOVID-19 ikhandidethi AfriVac 2121 njengamanje isezingeni lesikalo esiphezulu. Ngaphezu kwezinyanga ezingu-18 Afrigen isihambe uguquko olukhulu ngokwesekwa kohlelokuxhumana lababambisani nabeluleki abenza loluHlelo. Sesikhulise amandla ethu nokukwazi ukuhlangabezana nezinga elihle eliphezulu lokuthuthukiswa komgomo we-mRNA, ukusiza injongo yokwakha ukukwazi okugcinekile kumaLMIC ukukhiqiza imigomo ye-mRNA.". I-Afrigen isisungulwe ngempumelelo uhlelo lokukhiqizwa kwemigomo yeCOVID-19 ngesikalo selebhu njengamanje kuyanyuka uhlelo ngezinga elifanele lokukhiqiza ngezigaba imigomo ukuthi isetshenziswe ngesigaba sesibili noma sokuqala sokuhlola kwezokwelapha kumazinga eGMP. Kuhlelo oluhambisanayo, i-Afrigen izoqhubeka nokwenza ukuqeqesha nokudlulisela ezobuchwepheshe kubabambisani bohlelokuxhumana. Abaxhasi badlala indima ebalulekile ekwesekeni uHlelo ngesamba soxhaso kuze kube yimanje ngezigidi ezingu-117 neFrance ekubeni ngeyokuqala ukuxhasa umsebenzi wokudlulisela ezobuchwepheshe be-mRNA. Ababekhona emhlanganweni bekungabamele abaphuma e-European Commission, iBelgium, iGermany kanye neNorway, iCanada, i-Africa Union, iNingizimu Afrika kanye ne-ELMA Foundation. UCaroline Delany, uMqondisi Jikelele weGlobal Affairs Canada waseSouthern and Eastern Afrika, uthe: "iCanada iqinisekisa ukuqgubeka nokweseka kwe-mRNA Technology Transfer Programme futhi siyajabula ukumemezela ukuqhubeka kokunikelwa kwezigidi ezingu-15 ngamadola aseCanada emhlanganweni namhlanje. Lokhu kuletha iminikelo eyisamba yeCanada kuHlelo lwezigidi ezingu-45 kumadola aseCanada.
translations.hypothesis: प्रोफेसर पेट्रो टेर्ब्लांच, निर्देशक कार्यकारी अफ्रिगेन, भन्न: "पूरा टोली अफ्रिगेन उत्साहित छ, र यस महत्त्वपूर्ण उपलब्धिलाई हासिल गरेका छन्, यही अफ्रिगेन, mRNA प्रविधि प्लेटफर्मको पूर्णता। यो प्लेटफर्म अवस्थित छ, र यो mRNA केन्द्रको COVID-19 खोप, AfriVac 2121, विकास र उत्पादनको सुविधा, र यो हालै स्केल-अप चरणमा छ। मागील 18 महिना, अफ्रिगेनले अद्भुत परिवर्तन भोगेको छ, यस कार्यक्रमको माध्यमबाट साझेदार र गुरुहरूले मद्दत गरेको छ। हामीले हाम्रो क्षमता र capacidade बढाएका छौँ, र mRNA खोप विकासको उच्चतम মানको आवश्यकता धान्न, उद्देश्य यो छ, LMICs मा स्थायी क्षमता निर्माण गर्न mRNA खोप उत्पादन"। अफ्रिगेनले प्रयोगको प्रयोगशाला स्केलमा COVID-19 खोप उत्पादन प्रक्रिया सफलतापूर्वक स्थापना गरेको छ, र यो प्रक्रिया हालै, GMP मानको प्रयोगको खोप मात्रा उत्पादनको लागि उपयुक्त स्तरमा स्केल अप गरिएको छ। एकातिर, अफ्रिगेनले प्रशिक्षण र प्रविधि हस्तान्तरणलाई नेटवर्क साझेदारहरूलाई लगातार लागू गर्ने। दाताहरूले महत्त्वपूर्ण भूमिका खेल्छन्, र कार्यक्रमलाई पूर्ण रूपमा समर्थन, आजसम्म 117 मिलियन अमेरिकी डलर। फ्रान्स पहिलो देश हो којаले mRNA प्रविधि हस्तान्तरणको कामलाई समर्थन गरेको छ। बैठकमा, प्रतिनिधिहरूबाट युरोपियन आयोग, बेल्जियम, जर्मनी, साथै नर्वे, क्यानाडा, अफ्रिकन युनियन, दक्षिण अफ्रिका, र ELMA फाउन्डेसन। करेलाइन डेलानी, ग्लोबलअफेअर्स क्यानाडाका निर्देशक, दक्षिण र पूर्वी अफ्रिकाले भन्न: "क्यानाडा यस mRNA प्रविधि हस्तान्तरण कार्यक्रमको लगातार समर्थनलाई पुष्टिकरण, र हामी आजको बैठकमा थप 15 मिलियन क्यानाडा डलर योगदानको घोषणा गरेका छौँ। यसले क्यानाडाको कुल योगदानलाई 45 मिलियन क्यानाडा डलरसम्म पुर्‍याउँछ।

translations.source: These funds support the running of the Hub in South Africa as well as network partners around the world. We firmly believe in the importance of building capacity at local and regional level”. Mr Martin Seychell, Deputy Executive Director European Commission, said. “The EC has already contributed €40 million to the establishment of the mRNA technology transfer Hub and has recently signed another grant with the EIB for €15,5 million to facilitate the expansion of vaccines manufacturing capacity. This is part of the overall investment under the EU Global Gateway strategy, where more than €1 Billion have been mobilised under the Team Europe Initiative on Manufacturing health products. It is now particularly important to also address possible regulatory and demand bottlenecks, to ensure not only production of vaccines of the highest standard in all regions of the world, but also rapid and equitable access to those vaccines.”
translations.reference: Lokhu kwesekwa koxhasomali ekusebenzeni kweHabhu eNingizimu Afrika kanye nababambisani bohlelokuxhumana emhlabeni wonke. Siyakhulwa kakhulu ekubalulekeni kokwakha ukukwazi ezingeni lendawo nelesifunda". UMnu Martin Seychell, uSekela Mqondisi omkhulu we-European Commission esho. "I-EC isivele ixhase ngezigidi ezingu-€40 ukusungula ihabhu yokudlulisela yezobuchwepheshe kweMRNA futhi isanda kusayina esinye isibonelelo nge-EIB kwezigidi ezingu-€15.5 ukusiza ukwandisa amandla okukhiqizwa kwemigomo. Lokhu yingxenye yotshalomali lonke ngaphansi kwesu le EU Global Gateway, lapho ngaphezu kwesigidigidi esisodwa sama-euro selihlanganisiwe ngaphansi komzamo weTeam Europe ekukhiqizweni kwemikhiqizo yezempilo. Manje sekubalulekile ukuphinde sibhekane nokulawula okungenzeka nokukhona okufunakalyo, ukuqinisekisa ukuthiakukhona ukukhiqiza kuphela kwemigomo esezingeni eliphezulu kuzona zonke izifunda emhlabeni kodwa nokuphindeka kokutholakala okulinganayo kwaleyo migomo."
translations.hypothesis: यी स्रोतले, समर्थन कार्यान्वयनको केन्द्र, र साझेदार नेटवर्क विश्वव्यापी रूपमा। हामी दृढ विश्वास छौं मा स्थानीय र क्षेत्रीय स्तरमा क्षमता निर्माण।”, भन्यो श्री मार्टिन सेयшель, उप-कार्यकारी निर्देशक, युरोपियन आयोग। "युरोपियन आयोगले पहिले नै 40 मिलियन युरो योगदान गरेको छ, ताकि mRNA प्रविधि हस्तान्तरण केन्द्र स्थापित भइसकन, र हालै 15.5 मिलियन युरोको अर्को अनुदान हस्ताक्षर गरेको छ, ताकि खोप उत्पादन क्षमता विस्तार गर्न मद्दत गर सकू। यो EU ग्लोबल गेटवे रणनीति अन्तर्गतको कुल लगानी हो, जसमा 1 बिलियन युरो, Team Europe Initiative अन्तर्गत, स्वास्थ्य उत्पादन निर्माणको क्षेत्रमा जुटाइएको छ। अब, विशेष रूपमा, यो महत्त्वपूर्ण छ, सम्भावित नियमन र मागको बाधा हटाउन, ताकि न केवल विश्वव्यापी क्षेत्रहरूमा, उच्चतम মানको खोप उत्पादन सुनिश्चित भइसकन, तर साथै ती खोपहरूमा, तेज़ी र न्यायोचित पहुँच सुनिश्चित भइसकन।

translations.source: Key leadership appointments made to drive WHO strategic direction and initiatives The World Health Organization (WHO) has appointed five new senior figures to its headquarters leadership team in Geneva. The new appointments follow the reappointment of Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus to his second five-year term as Director-General. The overall leadership team has been consolidated to align with the Organization’s priorities for the next five years and will work closely with the Director-General to drive forward these priorities and WHO’s ambitious transformation agenda.
